passages.passage_text: Read the following information to answer the questions given below. From a group of six boys M, N, O, P, Q and R and five girls G, H, I, J and K a team of six is to be selected. Some of the criteria of selection are as follows I. M and J go together. II. O cannot be placed with N. III. I cannot go with J. IV. N goes with H. V. P and Q have to be together. VI. K and R go together. VII. Unless otherwise stated these criteria are applicable to all the following questions.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the team consists of two girls and I is one of them, the other members are

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the team has four boys including O and R, the members of the team other than O and R are

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If four members are boys, which of the following cannot constitute the team?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If both K and P are members of the team and three boys in all are included in the team, the members of the team other than K and P are

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the team has three girls including J and K, the members of the team other than J and K are

solutions.solution: In this question, option (a) violates condition (i), option (b) violates condition (i), option (c) violates condition (i), option (d) complies with all the conditions.
If D is chosen, C cannot be chosen. So, alternatives (c) and (d) are rejected. If A is chosen, B has to be chosen, so alternative (b) is rejected. Option (a) complies with all the conditions.
Alternatives (a), (b) and (c) violate condition (i). Option (d) complies with all the conditions.

With M, J is necessary i.e., (a) is not possible. Either N or O is selected. Hence, (c) is not possible. With N, H is necessary. Hence, (d) is not possible. Only option (b) complies with all the conditions.
With R, K is necessary. Hence, (a), (b) and (d) are not possible.
O cannot be with N. Hence, (b) is not possible.

Since, H and N must be together, so (b) is not possible. P and Q must be together. Hence, (c) is not possible. Since, I cannot go with J, hence (d) is not possible. Only option (a) complies with all the conditions.
G, H, J and K are four girls, so (a) is not possible. O and N do not want to be together. So, (c) and (d) are not possible. Only option (b) complies with all the conditions.
